item.title: Unstable building in Midtown forces multiple evacuations, street closures
item.description: The unstable building is the former Pfizer headquarters in Midtown Manhattan that is being converted into apartments.

item.title: Ford recalls 177,000 vehicles over faulty wiper, drivetrain and other issues
item.description: A slew of Ford recalls affects some new and used Mustang, Lincoln Nautilus Hybrid and Explorer Hybrid vehicles, according to a federal safety watchdog.

item.title: U.S. revokes license authorizing sale of Iranian oil after tankers attacked
item.description: As many as three tankers came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Iran continued exchanging threats, with peace talks on hold.

item.title: Charlie Kirk murder suspect changed clothes before shooting, agent says
item.description: An investigator testified that a video shows Tyler Robinson going over a railing onto a rooftop, crouching down and running to a site overlooking where Charlie Kirk was speaking​.

item.title: Trump weighs allowing Turkey to have F-35s as NATO summit begins
item.description: Amid simmering tensions between the U.S. and some NATO allies over Iran and Greenland, President Trump is tightening bonds with Turkey.

item.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
item.description: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: How much interest can a 3-year CD account earn now?
item.description: A 3-year CD account will result in a big return (and extended protection through today's economic volatility).

item.title: Prosecutors in LA announce charges against Indian syndicate "Bishnoi"
item.description: An indictment announced in Los Angeles accuses Lawrence Bishnoi and another defendant of ordering the killing of "H.S.N." — the apparent initials for Hardeep Singh Nijjar​, who was gunned down​ on June 18, 2023, near a Sikh temple in Surrey, British Columbia​.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Wildfire detection satellites aim to spot destructive fires from space
item.description: FireSat satellites, equipped with specialized cameras and imaging software, are helping detect wildfires from space. Muon Space CEO Jonny Dyer explains how the satellites could help firefighting efforts.

item.title: Democrats question Trump-linked businesses over IRS settlement's implications
item.description: Senate Democrats including Elizabeth Warren, Ron Wyden, and Chuck Schumer are pressing businesses linked to President Trump about his settlement with the IRS barring the agency from pursuing certain tax claims against him, questioning whether it covers affiliated firms. Jake Rosen reports.

item.title: Senate GOP leaders say they've talked with McConnell by phone
item.description: The top Republicans in the Senate spoke with the Kentucky Republican by phone this week, according to their spokespeople.

item.title: USPS raising the price of a Forever stamp starting on Sunday
item.description: The cost of a first-class Forever stamp has climbed 41% since 2021, and postal officials have signaled they want prices to rise even more.

item.title: How much interest will a $35,000 high-yield savings account earn over the next year?
item.description: If you have $35,000 to move into a high-yield savings account, it helps to start with the interest-earning potential.

item.title: Mexico probing possible U.S. violation in drug lord capture: "Someone lied"
item.description: Mexico said it is investigating whether its sovereignty was violated by the United States in the 2024 capture of drug lord Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada.

item.title: Court expected to hear key recorded statement at Charlie Kirk murder hearing
item.description: Charlie Kirk's alleged killer, Tyler Robinson, is back in a Utah court for a preliminary hearing. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has more details.

item.title: Sen. Bernie Sanders says he told Graham Platner to end his campaign after sexual assault allegation
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders says he told Graham Platner to drop out of his Senate race in Maine after a woman accused Platner of sexual assault. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $90,000 long-term CD account will earn if opened this July
item.description: Interest earnings on a CD account of this size will be substantial, but that's not the only benefit for savers now.

item.title: Sen. Dick Durbin reacts to Trump's plan to lift Turkey sanctions
item.description: Illinois Democratic Sen. Dick Durbin joins CBS News 24/7 to discuss President Trump's announcement at the NATO summit that he's lifting U.S. sanctions on Turkey.

item.title: Sen. Bernie Sanders says he encouraged Graham Platner to step aside
item.description: Independent Sen. Bernie Sanders of Vermont said Tuesday that he spoke with Maine Democratic Senate nominee Graham Platner and recommended that he exit the Senate race.

item.title: Rahm Emanuel expected to say Israel needs "significant changes" in speech
item.description: Rahm Emanuel​ is viewed as a potential Democratic presidential candidate and has been a longtime defender of Israel.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: Here's a look at how events have unfolded in the Karen Read case.

item.title: Big explosions in Syria as Macron visits
item.description: Syrian state TV is reporting that at least 18 people were injured after explosions hit the Syrian capital of Damascus during a visit by French President Emmanuel Macron. Chris Livesay reports.

item.title: U.K. far-right leader Nigel Farage resigns as lawmaker in risky gamble
item.description: Populist British lawmaker Nigel Farage has resigned amid a financial scandal, but says he'll run in a snap election to reclaim his seat.

item.title: Video shows "well fed" great white shark eating whale off Rhode Island
item.description: A great white shark was captured feeding on the carcass of a humpback whale near Rhode Island, in rare video footage.

item.title: Trump meeting with NATO leaders including Erdoğan, Zelenskyy
item.description: President Trump is meeting with various world leaders at the NATO summit in Turkey, including Turkish President Recep Erdoğan and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe and former CIA officer Elliot Ackerman comment on the political agendas for Mr. Trump's meetings.

item.title: Walmart cuts prices on thousands of items, including beef and soda
item.description: President Trump praised Walmart and said the lower prices are the result of "my Administration's request to celebrate our great Country's 250th birthday."

item.title: Nara Organics infant formula linked to another case of botulism
item.description: The FDA is urging parents and caregivers to immediately stop using a Nara Organics-brand formula after several infants contracted botulism.

item.title: Top Democrats call on Platner to drop out as sexual assault allegation emerges
item.description: A Maine woman told Politico that Graham Platner entered her home in late 2021 and forced himself on her. Platner denies the allegation.

item.title: 2,000-year-old gold rings and human bones found by archaeologists
item.description: Archaeologists have discovered eight human skeletons, bronze and gold jewelry and other artifacts indicating a ceremonial burial of wealthy people.
